User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the MisterTuga Insights application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

role.description: Role of the user (e.g., ADMIN, FORNECEDOR).

ProfitStat.title: ProfitStat
ProfitStat.description: Represents profit statistics data for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the profit stat entry.

date.description: Date for which the profit stat is recorded.

profit.description: Profit amount for the recorded date.

definition.description: Stores user profile information.  Authorization is based on matching request.auth.uid to the userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores profit statistics for a given user. Authorization is based on matching request.auth.uid to the userId parent document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user to which the profit stat belongs.

params.name: profitStatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the profit stat entry.

definition.description: Documents in this collection signify that the user has an ADMIN role. Existence of the document is sufficient; content is ignored.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is an admin.

firestore.reasoning: This Firestore structure prioritizes security and scalability by adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling. User data and profit statistics are stored in separate collections to ensure clear ownership and access control. The structure leverages path-based ownership for user-related data, making security rules straightforward. Specifically, the `/users/{userId}` collection stores user profiles. The `/users/{userId}/profitStats/{profitStatId}` subcollection stores profit statistics associated with each user, maintaining a clear 1:N relationship and making ownership explicit through the path. This design eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules because authorization is based solely on the `request.auth.uid` matching the `userId` in the path, ensuring Authorization Independence. It facilitates secure list operations (QAPs) as rules can easily filter based on path-based ownership without scanning unrelated data. The structure allows to create atomic operations using transactions and batches.
